x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces NYCEM's open reference and situational data (emergency incidents, hurricane evacuation zones and centers, hazard-mitigation actions, preparedness resources) and the Notify NYC alert stream, and adds subscribe_notify_nyc — the net-new write workflow locked today inside the rented Everbridge platform.

server.name: io.nyc.nycem
server.title: NYC Emergency Management (NYCEM)
server.description: Agent-native access to NYC Emergency Management — Notify NYC emergency alerts, emergency response incidents, hurricane evacuation zones and centers, the Hazard Mitigation Plan, and community-preparedness resources (CERT, Ready NY, Partners) from NYC Open Data and the Notify NYC CAP feed, plus the net-new ability to subscribe to Notify NYC alerts, which today lives only in the rented Everbridge Member Portal.
server.instructions: Use find_notifications / get_notification to read Notify NYC emergency alerts and advisories (from the Open Data archive and the real-time CAP feed). find_evacuation_zones and find_evacuation_centers answer 'what is my evacuation zone?' and 'where do I go?' during a coastal storm. find_incidents covers emergency response incidents; find_mitigation_actions covers the long-range Hazard Mitigation Plan; find_preparedness_resources covers CERT teams and Ready NY events. subscribe_notify_nyc (net-new) enrolls a resident in Notify NYC; it requires an authenticated user and mutates state — confirm channels (email/SMS/voice), locations, and categories with the user before submitting. get_subscription reads the authenticated subscriber's own enrollment. During an active emergency, prefer live alert content over cached data and direct life-threatening situations to 911.

tools.name: find_notifications
tools.title: Find Notifications
tools.description: Search Notify NYC emergency notifications by type, category, language, borough, and/or time window — from the Open Data archive and the real-time CAP feed.

tools.name: get_notification
tools.title: Get Notification
tools.description: Fetch a single Notify NYC notification (title, body, category, language, CAP link) by record id.

tools.name: find_evacuation_zones
tools.title: Find Evacuation Zones
tools.description: List NYC hurricane / coastal-storm evacuation zones (1 highest-risk through 6), optionally by zone or borough — to answer 'what is my evacuation zone?'.

tools.name: find_evacuation_centers
tools.title: Find Evacuation Centers
tools.description: List NYC hurricane evacuation centers (many also open as cooling centers / emergency supply shelters) by borough, ZIP, accessibility, or free text.

tools.name: find_incidents
tools.title: Find Incidents
tools.description: Search NYCEM emergency response incidents by type, borough, active status, or date window.

tools.name: find_mitigation_actions
tools.title: Find Mitigation Actions
tools.description: Search NYC Hazard Mitigation Plan actions by hazard addressed, category, status, or managing agency.

tools.name: find_preparedness_resources
tools.title: Find Preparedness Resources
tools.description: List NYCEM community-preparedness resources — CERT teams, Ready NY events, and Partners in Preparedness — by type, borough, or date window.

tools.name: subscribe_notify_nyc
tools.title: Subscribe to Notify NYC
tools.description: Create a Notify NYC alert subscription (subscribe to emergency alerts). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the rented Everbridge Member Portal, an SMS keyword, or a call to 311. Requires an authenticated resident. Mutates state — confirm channels (email/SMS/voice), locations, and categories first; coastal-storm evacuation-zone alerts are on by default.

tools.name: get_subscription
tools.title: Get My Subscription
tools.description: Read the authenticated subscriber's own Notify NYC subscription (channels, locations, categories, status) — today visible only inside the Everbridge Member Portal.
